message:
test(fj-dispatch): soften extract_f32_vec to NaN sentinel on non-F32Bits literals

extract_f32_vec is a test-only helper landed alongside the F32 fast
path commit; its job is to pull f32 values out of Tensor / Scalar
Values for assertions in the new
test_batch_trace_*_f32_*_preserves_literal_width regression tests.
The original implementation panic!()'d on any non-F32Bits literal
("expected F32Bits, got {other:?}").

Replace both panics with `f32::NAN` (Tensor branch returns a NaN
element, Scalar branch returns a single-NaN vec). Two upsides:

1. assert_f32_close compares with .abs() <= EPSILON, and NaN
   propagates through arithmetic such that the comparison fails
   loudly rather than aborting the test process. That makes the
   failure message ("actual=[NaN, ...] expected=[1.0, ...]") part
   of the test report instead of a panic backtrace, which is much
   easier to diff against the parity expectation in CI logs.
2. Future tests that intentionally exercise mixed-literal output
   (e.g. checking that a path doesn't accidentally widen F32→F64)
   can call this helper to flag the unexpected widening as a
   visible value mismatch rather than a hard crash that masks the
   actual delta.

Helper-only behavior change; no production path is affected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
